mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% is near zero, indicating an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce real yields, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline as investors seek duration. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate regimes.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of 20-day forward periods despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.7% signals labor market slack. When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, the Fed maintains accommodative policy, reducing long-term yield expectations. The mechanism triggers when unemployment rises above this threshold, signaling weaker demand and lower future rates.
regime_hint: labor market slack with policy accommodation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 6.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and 20-day forward yield changes is positive (r > 0.1) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 433,907 million dollars combined with housing starts at 907 thousand units indicate consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, demand pressures build, forcing the Fed to signal tighter policy and raising long-term Treasury yields through inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 55% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST grow > 2% MoM, or the joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.63 (index 2017=100) near trend indicates stable supply conditions. When INDPRO declines below 101.0, supply constraints emerge, raising inflation expectations and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market prices in persistent price pressures.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 60% of 60-day forward periods when INDPRO < 101.0, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 235.64 (1982-1984=100) reflects current inflation levels. When CPI growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, inflation expectations rise, forcing the Fed to signal rate increases and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher through both inflation and term premium channels.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 58% of 20-day forward periods when CPI growth > 0.3% MoM, or the correlation between CPI momentum and yield changes is negative (r < -0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 137,928 thousand persons combined with unemployment at 6.7% creates a dual signal. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month while UNRATE remains elevated, labor market momentum weakens, signaling recession risk and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as safe-haven demand increases.
regime_hint: labor market deceleration with recession signals
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 62% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in rolling 12-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 15,942.3 billion chained 2017 dollars (6-month lag) represents the broadest demand measure. When quarterly GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized, growth expectations fall, reducing inflation expectations and pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower as the market reprices growth and rate paths.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 58% of 60-day forward periods when GDP growth < 2% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero FEDFUNDS (0.08%) and elevated CPI (235.64) creates negative real rates. When real rates remain deeply negative (FEDFUNDS - CPI inflation < -2%), financial conditions remain loose, suppressing long-term yields as investors expect sustained accommodation. This interaction-driven mechanism captures the joint effect of monetary and price dynamics.
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPI inflation is not statistically significant (p > 0.11) in rolling 12-month regressions.
